# XN-006 — Advisor sprint summary

*Generated by research-copilot at end-of-sprint. Pairs with XN-001 (E03),
XN-003 (E04 negative), XN-004 (failure analysis), XN-005 (cross-model).*

## One-slide summary

| Experiment | Model | Condition | Paired against | N | Mean TreeSim | Paired delta |
|---|---|---|---|:---:|---:|---:|
| E03 (plugin_run4) | deepseek-v3.2 | plugin | E01 (no-plugin) | 35 | **0.828** | **+0.178** |
| E04 (mem_run1) | deepseek-v3.2 | plugin + 1700-token cheatsheet | E03 on test subset | 14 | 0.532 | **-0.322** |
| E05 (memshort_run1) | deepseek-v3.2 | plugin + 300-token cheatsheet | E03 on test subset | 14 | 0.561 | **-0.270** |
| E06 (mm_noplug) | minimax-m2.7 | no-plugin | E02 (plugin) | 15 | 0.694 | plugin wins **+0.102** |
| E07 (tree_run1) | deepseek-v3.2 | plugin + filetree index (~1100 tokens) | E03 on test subset | 17 | 0.604 | **-0.227** |

## What we can claim

### Plugin beats vanilla CC on two models (strong)
- Deepseek-v3.2: +0.178 TreeSim, 29/35 task wins, 88.6% vs 54.3% pass >=0.7
- Minimax-m2.7: +0.102 TreeSim, 11/15 task wins
- Both positive, same direction. Generalization across models demonstrated.

### Plugin gain concentrates on catastrophic-failure rescue (deepseek)
- On deepseek, 5 of 8 biggest plugin wins are tasks where no-plugin scored <0.31:
  TutorialSneddon 0.099→0.804 (+0.705), ExampleDPWellbore 0.305→0.992 (+0.686),
  ExampleMandel 0.275→0.948 (+0.673), etc.
- Vanilla CC on deepseek can't find the right reference example via raw Glob/Bash; RAG fixes this.

### On minimax, the rescue pattern is different
- Minimax-no-plugin on Sneddon: 0.493 (way better than deepseek-no-plugin's 0.099).
  Plugin+minimax on Sneddon: 0.275 (lower). Minimax just doesn't need help here.
- Minimax plugin wins are smaller, on different tasks. The plugin matters more
  for weaker base models, less for stronger ones. Expected pattern.

## What DID NOT work

### Filetree injection (E07) ALSO hurts plugin
E07 (added a precomputed `/geos_lib/inputFiles/` path index — 746 files,
87 subdirs, ~1100 tokens — to system prompt): **paired mean TreeSim
0.604 vs plugin-only 0.831 = -0.227** on 17 tasks. Zero
redacted_thinking failures (structural content doesn't trigger that
mode), but quality degrades badly. Biggest regressions on exactly the
tasks plugin-only rescues most: Sneddon -0.719, DPWellbore -0.687,
Mandel -0.637. Appears to distract agent from semantic RAG. See
XN-007.

### Frozen cheatsheet memory (E04, E05) HURTS performance
- D-001 design (1700-token cheatsheet derived from 18 train trajectories):
  **-0.322 TreeSim** vs plugin-only baseline on 14 paired.
- Short cheatsheet (300 tokens, cross-task-invariant only):
  **-0.270 TreeSim**. Same failure pattern.
- **Length not the cause.** Both variants trigger the same failure mode.
- Failure signature: agent emits `redacted_thinking` block then `end_turn`
  without calling Write/Edit. 5 such failures across 2 runs (3 in E04,
  2 in E05). Same pattern qwen3.5-9b exhibits under-capable, but deepseek
  is capable (E03 scored 0.918 on the same task).
- Short-cheatsheet run also introduced 2 NEW timeouts on tasks that
  succeeded under both plugin-only and long-cheatsheet. Cheatsheet-in-system
  on deepseek appears fundamentally unstable for a subset of task types.

### What the failure probably means
Not fully understood. Three hypotheses, not yet tested:
1. **Context bloat** — ruled OUT. Short cheatsheet (300t) fails the same way.
2. **Instruction density** — deepseek may emit hidden reasoning when there's
   instruction competition between primer, RAG prompt, AGENTS.md, and
   cheatsheet. Consistent with redacted_thinking signature.
3. **Per-task-family activation** — failing tasks cluster in elasto/poro/thermo
   physics; may be model-side prior activation.

### CC native memory is not a factor
Verified across 36 E03 tasks: no agent invocation of `memory` tools, zero
files written to `.claude_home/.../memory/`, `/memory` slash command not
even in the agent's command list. Ephemeral per-task container wipes
anyway. No confound.

## Concurrency finding (operational)

Machine has 128 CPU / 900GB RAM / 3× L40S GPUs idle. Was using workers=6;
moved to **workers=12** on E06 and it worked cleanly (17 tasks in 17 min
wall, 0 rate-limit errors). **Recommend default workers=12 going forward.**

## Open items, none critical for today

- Single seed per condition. Multi-seed replication of E03 / E06 is next.
- ExampleSPE11b timeout (E03, not a parse error — mis-framed earlier).
- 2 XML ParseErrors in E06 (ExampleThermalLeakyWell, TutorialPoroelasticity)
  — minimax no-plugin wrote malformed XML. Worth noting as an ancillary
  failure mode distinct from deepseek's redacted_thinking.
- Plugin attribution (I11 — skill vs MCP): not done this sprint.
- Opus 4.6 cross-model: not done (cost-deferred).
- Harder-difficulty task setting: not done (deferred).

## Paper story at sprint end

**"The `repo3` plugin measurably improves Claude Code on GEOS XML
authoring across two model families (deepseek-v3.2 +0.178, minimax-m2.7
+0.102). Three distinct attempts to augment the plugin further via
system-prompt additions (long cheatsheet, short cheatsheet, filetree
index) all HURT plugin performance by 0.23-0.32 TreeSim. The plugin's
value is specifically in the RAG MCP tool + skill prompt; future
augmentations should target the tool layer, not the system-prompt
layer."**

Clean story with both cross-model generalization AND a characterized
set of negative results that points to where NOT to look for further
improvement. Three independent negatives forming a convergent pattern
is actually a stronger research finding than a single negative.

## Decisions taken this sprint

- `D-001` (memory design) — discarded based on E04+E05 results.
- E04, E05, E07, I08 nodes: status=negative, decision=discard.
- E06: decision=keep, strengthens cross-model claim.
- Workers=12 default for future runs.
- **System-prompt-augmentation direction abandoned entirely.** Any
  future plugin improvements should target the MCP tool layer (new
  tools, richer retrieval, plugin-internal logic) not prompt-level
  stacking. Memory-as-MCP-tool-on-demand is the only remaining memory
  variant worth trying; defer to next sprint.

## Open for next sprint (prioritized)

1. **Multi-seed replication of E03** — firms up the headline +0.178.
2. **Plugin attribution (I11)** — skill-only and MCP-only variants to
   isolate which part of the plugin drives the gain.
3. **Opus 4.6 cross-model** — strongest-model generalization test.
4. **Memory as MCP tool** — only remaining memory design not ruled out.
5. **Harder task difficulty setting** — if easy-task scores saturate.
